breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Attack Compromises LiteLLM and Trivy on PyPI
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actor TeamPCP has expanded its supply chain attack campaign by compromising the popular LiteLLM Python package on PyPI, following earlier breaches of Trivy and KICS. Malicious versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 were injected with credential harvesters targeting SSH keys, cloud tokens, and Kubernetes secrets before being removed from the repository.

This incident impacts thousands of organizations relying on AI pipeline tools, potentially exposing cloud infrastructure and CI/CD credentials to attackers. The campaign highlights the growing risk of open-source dependencies serving as gateways into enterprise environments, prompting urgent credential rotation advisories from PyPI and security vendors.

breaking_news.title: CISA Flags Langflow RCE Vulnerability Actively Exploited by Attackers
breaking_news.summary:
CISA has added a critical vulnerability in the Langflow framework, CVE-2026-33017, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after attackers weaponized it within hours of disclosure. The flaw allows unauthenticated remote code execution on AI workflow instances by exploiting a public build endpoint that executes user-supplied Python code without sandboxing.

Active exploitation has been observed across multiple cloud providers, with attackers attempting to exfiltrate environment variables and hijack AI agents. Organizations using Langflow for agentic AI workflows face immediate risk of full system compromise and must apply mitigations while awaiting vendor patches.

breaking_news.title: Red Menshen APT Deploys Stealthy BPFDoor Implants in Telecom Networks
breaking_news.summary:
Rapid7 Labs uncovered a long-term espionage campaign by the China-linked Red Menshen APT group, which deployed stealthy BPFDoor implants within telecom networks across the Middle East and Asia. These kernel-level backdoors allow attackers to maintain persistent, hidden access to critical infrastructure for prolonged surveillance of government communications.

The strategic positioning within telecom backbones threatens entire populations by compromising call records and digital identities carried by these networks. This sophisticated intrusion underscores the severity of state-sponsored threats targeting essential communication infrastructure for intelligence gathering.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Linked to Operation Triangulation Campaign
breaking_news.summary:
Kaspersky researchers discovered that the Coruna iOS exploit kit reuses updated kernel exploit code from the 2023 Operation Triangulation campaign, linking it to a highly sophisticated espionage framework. The kit targets Apple iPhones running iOS versions 13.0 through 17.2.1 using zero-click iMessage exploits and multiple exploit chains.

This evolution of the Triangulation framework suggests a continued threat to high-value targets using Apple devices, with capabilities to bypass security controls without user interaction. The discovery raises significant concerns about the persistence of advanced mobile threats and the need for immediate updates to the latest iOS releases.

breaking_news.title: FBI Confirms Iran-Linked Hackers Stole Director Kash Patel's Personal Emails
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI has confirmed that an Iran-linked hacking group successfully stole personal emails from the account of FBI Director Kash Patel, claiming credit for the breach on dark web forums. While the agency stated the information is historical and contains no government secrets, the incident represents a significant compromise of a high-profile law enforcement official.

This attack highlights the increasing targeting of government leadership by state-sponsored actors seeking to gather intelligence or create reputational damage. The breach necessitates immediate security reviews for other senior officials and reinforces the need for robust personal account protection against sophisticated phishing campaigns.

breaking_news.title: Citrix Urges Patching Critical NetScaler Flaw Allowing Unauthenticated Data Leaks
breaking_news.summary:
Citrix has issued urgent patches for a critical vulnerability, CVE-2026-3055, in NetScaler ADC and Gateway devices that allows unauthenticated attackers to leak sensitive data from appliance memory. The flaw is an out-of-bounds read issue similar in severity to the widely exploited CitrixBleed vulnerabilities, posing a severe risk to identity and authentication configurations.

Security experts warn that leaving this flaw unpatched could lead to the exposure of credentials and sensitive information without requiring any prior access to the system. Organizations managing NetScaler devices configured as SAML IDPs must apply updates immediately to prevent potential memory scraping attacks.

breaking_news.title: CISA and German Police Warn of Critical PTC Windchill RCE Vulnerability
breaking_news.summary:
CISA and German police have warned organizations of a critical remote code execution flaw, CVE-2026-4681, in PTC Windchill and FlexPLM software with a CVSS score of 10.0. The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code through deserialization of untrusted data, and no patch is currently available despite imminent exploitation risks.

This defect impacts product lifecycle management systems used by major manufacturing and engineering firms, potentially allowing full control over critical design data and infrastructure. Authorities have mobilized to physically warn organizations in Germany, emphasizing the urgency of applying vendor mitigations before patches are released.

breaking_news.title: European Commission Investigating Breach After Amazon Cloud Account Hack
breaking_news.summary:
The European Commission is investigating a security breach after a threat actor gained unauthorized access to its Amazon cloud infrastructure, compromising executive body operations. The incident involves a threat actor infiltrating cloud accounts, raising concerns about the security of sensitive EU government data stored on third-party platforms.

This breach impacts the core administrative functions of the European Union and could expose confidential policy documents or citizen data. It underscores the vulnerabilities inherent in cloud dependency for government entities and triggers a high-level investigation into access controls and identity management.

breaking_news.title: Stryker Confirms Iranian Cyber Actors Wiped 200,000 Devices in Malware Attack
breaking_news.summary:
Medical device manufacturer Stryker confirmed that Iranian cyber actors wiped more than 200,000 company devices during a destructive malware attack, forcing production lines to temporarily reopen. The FBI has issued alerts regarding the specific malware used by these state-sponsored hackers to disrupt critical healthcare infrastructure operations.

The attack demonstrates the escalating geopolitical cyber conflict targeting essential industries, with potential risks to patient care and supply chain continuity. Stryker's incident serves as a warning to other healthcare and manufacturing firms about the threat of destructive wiper malware from nation-state adversaries.

breaking_news.title: Lapsus$ Claims Credit for Hacking Pharma Giant AstraZeneca
breaking_news.summary:
The notorious extortion group Lapsus$ has claimed responsibility for hacking pharmaceutical giant AstraZeneca, alleging the theft of 3GB of data including credentials and internal code repositories. While the company has not yet confirmed the breach, the listing on dark web forums suggests a significant compromise of employee information and proprietary software code.

If verified, this incident represents one of the most serious healthcare cyber breaches of the year, potentially enabling further phishing or supply chain attacks against the pharma giant. The claim highlights the continued activity of Lapsus$ and the ongoing risk to intellectual property in the life sciences sector.
